title: The Hidden Energy Crisis: How AI and Climate Change Strain Power Grids
subtitle: As tech companies expand their AI data centers, record-breaking heat and environmental stress are putting new pressure on global electricity supplies.

summary.en: Major technology companies are facing rising carbon emissions due to the rapid growth of AI data centers. At the same time, extreme weather is making it harder for power plants to keep up with electricity demand. Unusually warm rivers in Europe are forcing nuclear plants to lower their output to protect the environment. Meanwhile, powerful storms like Typhoon Bavi are causing massive disruptions across Asia. These events highlight the growing conflict between digital expansion and a changing climate.
summary.zh: 由於人工智慧資料中心的快速成長，大型科技公司正面臨碳排放量上升的問題。與此同時，極端氣候使發電廠難以滿足電力需求。歐洲異常溫暖的河水迫使核電廠降低發電量以保護環境。與此同時，像巴威颱風這樣的強烈風暴正在亞洲各地造成大規模破壞。這些事件凸顯了數位擴張與氣候變遷之間日益嚴重的衝突。

paragraphs.en: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence is changing how we use energy, but this digital progress comes with a physical cost. As reported in recent site coverage, major technology companies like Microsoft, Amazon, and Google have seen their carbon emissions rise significantly. This increase is largely tied to the construction of massive data centers, which are buildings filled with powerful computers needed to support AI. These facilities require huge amounts of electricity and water to operate, placing a heavy burden on local power grids.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧的快速成長正在改變我們使用能源的方式，但這種數位進步伴隨著實體的代價。根據近期報導，微軟 (Microsoft)、亞馬遜 (Amazon) 和 Google 等大型科技公司的碳排放量顯著上升。這種成長主要與興建大型資料中心有關，這些建築內裝滿了支援人工智慧所需的強大電腦。這些設施在運作時需要消耗大量的電力與水資源，對當地的電力網路造成沉重負擔。

paragraphs.en: This challenge is now meeting a new, dangerous obstacle: extreme weather. According to The Guardian, western and central Europe are currently experiencing unusually high temperatures and very little rain. This hot weather has created a difficult situation for energy infrastructure. In France, many nuclear power stations rely on river water to cool their systems. However, environmental rules prevent these plants from putting too much hot water back into the rivers, as this can harm the local ecosystem. When river temperatures rise, operators must limit the amount of electricity they produce.
paragraphs.zh: 這項挑戰目前正遭遇一個新的危險障礙：極端氣候。根據《衛報》(The Guardian) 報導，歐洲西部與中部目前正經歷異常高溫且降雨稀少的天氣。這種炎熱天氣為能源基礎設施帶來了嚴峻考驗。在法國，許多核能發電廠依賴河水來冷卻系統。然而，環保法規禁止這些電廠將過熱的水排回河川，因為這會傷害當地的生態系統。當河水溫度升高時，營運商必須限制發電量。

paragraphs.en: The Guardian noted that these limits are already having an impact. Earlier this month, the energy company EDF had to temporarily shut down a reactor at the Golfech nuclear power station because the Garonne River became too warm. Similar restrictions are expected at the Nogent nuclear power station. As temperatures rise, the demand for electricity also grows because more people are using air-conditioning to stay cool. This creates a difficult cycle where the need for power increases just as the ability to produce it becomes limited.
paragraphs.zh: 《衛報》指出，這些限制已經產生影響。本月稍早，法國電力公司 (EDF) 因加龍河 (Garonne River) 水溫過高，被迫暫時關閉戈爾費什 (Golfech) 核電廠的一座反應爐。諾讓 (Nogent) 核電廠預計也將面臨類似的限制。隨著氣溫升高，電力需求也隨之增加，因為更多人使用空調來降溫。這形成了一種惡性循環：在發電能力受限的同時，電力需求卻在增加。

paragraphs.en: While Europe deals with heat and dry conditions, other parts of the world are facing different environmental threats. In Asia, Typhoon Bavi recently moved across the region, bringing heavy rain and strong winds. Before hitting China, the storm grew stronger over very warm waters in the western Pacific. The Guardian reported that more than 2 million people were evacuated as the storm caused widespread transport delays and flooding. Even after weakening, the storm continues to pose a risk of landslides and flash flooding as it moves inland.
paragraphs.zh: 當歐洲應對高溫與乾旱的同時，世界其他地區也面臨不同的環境威脅。在亞洲，巴威 (Bavi) 颱風近期橫掃該地區，帶來強風豪雨。在登陸中國之前，這場風暴在西太平洋的溫暖海域中增強。據《衛報》報導，由於風暴造成廣泛的交通延誤與淹水，超過兩百萬人被迫撤離。即便風暴減弱，隨著它向內陸移動，仍持續帶來土石流與洪水風險。

paragraphs.en: These environmental pressures are not happening in isolation. As noted in previous reports, communities are increasingly concerned about the physical cost of AI infrastructure. Data centers are not only energy-intensive but also compete with local needs for water and power. When heatwaves or storms strike, the strain on the grid becomes even more visible. The combination of rising AI energy needs and a changing climate suggests that the future of power production will be increasingly complex.
paragraphs.zh: 這些環境壓力並非孤立發生。如先前報導所述，社區對於人工智慧基礎設施所帶來的實體代價日益擔憂。資料中心不僅耗能，還會與當地爭奪水資源與電力。當熱浪或風暴來襲時，電網承受的壓力變得更加明顯。人工智慧能源需求上升與氣候變遷的結合，顯示未來電力生產將會變得日益複雜。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the situation remains uncertain. Weather models indicate that warmer-than-average conditions will likely continue across southern Europe, keeping the risk of wildfires high and river temperatures elevated. At the same time, the tech industry continues to build more data centers to support the demand for AI. Experts are now questioning how these companies will manage their environmental impact while the world faces more frequent and intense weather events.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，局勢仍充滿不確定性。氣象模型顯示，南歐地區氣溫高於平均的情況可能會持續，導致野火風險居高不下，河水溫度也持續偏高。與此同時，科技產業仍持續興建更多資料中心以支援人工智慧需求。專家們現在質疑，當世界面臨更頻繁且劇烈的氣候事件時，這些公司將如何管理其對環境的影響。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the challenge is two-fold. First, the tech industry must find ways to make AI more energy-efficient to reduce its carbon footprint. Second, energy providers must find ways to keep power grids stable even when climate change makes traditional energy sources, like water-cooled nuclear plants, harder to operate. As the world becomes more digital, the need for a reliable and sustainable energy system has never been more important.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，這項挑戰包含兩個層面。首先，科技產業必須設法提高人工智慧的能源效率，以減少碳足跡。其次，能源供應商必須找到方法，即使在氣候變遷導致水冷式核電廠等傳統能源運作困難的情況下，仍能維持電網穩定。隨著世界變得更加數位化，建立一個可靠且永續的能源系統顯得前所未有的重要。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Caused by or directly connected to something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡表示因果關係，而非字面上的「綁在一起」。文中：This increase is largely tied to the construction of massive data centers, which are buildings filled with powerful computers needed to support AI.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A heavy load or a difficult responsibility that causes problems.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常指心理壓力，這裡指對電力系統造成的物理性負荷。文中：These facilities require huge amounts of electricity and water to operate, placing a heavy burden on local power grids.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To happen suddenly and cause damage or problems.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「打擊」，這裡指天氣災害突然發生。文中：When heatwaves or storms strike, the strain on the grid becomes even more visible.
由數字 two 和後綴 -fold 組成，表示結構上的雙重性。文中：Ultimately, the challenge is two-fold.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants

previousArticles.title: The Hidden Cost of AI: Tech Giants See Carbon Emissions Rise
previousArticles.summaryEn: Major technology companies like Microsoft, Amazon, and Google have seen their carbon emissions rise significantly over the past year. This increase is largely due to the rapid construction of data centers needed to support artificial intelligence. Experts argue that these companies are struggling to balance their climate promises with the massive energy demands of new digital tools. While the companies maintain their long-term goals for net-zero emissions, the current data shows a move in the opposite direction. The situation highlights a growing tension between the expansion of AI and global environmental sustainability efforts.

previousArticles.title: The Physical Cost of AI: Communities Fight Back Against Data Centers
previousArticles.summaryEn: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence is leading to a massive increase in the construction of energy-intensive data centers. These facilities require huge amounts of electricity and water, placing significant strain on local power grids. Communities across the United States and beyond are now organizing to block or delay these projects. Recent investigations reveal that some tech companies have misrepresented their environmental impact and sustainability plans. As these projects face more scrutiny, the future of the AI boom depends on how companies address these physical and environmental challenges.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason why French nuclear power stations must sometimes limit their electricity production during heatwaves?

options.A: The power plants lack the necessary staff to operate during extreme heat.
options.B: Environmental regulations restrict the temperature of water released back into rivers.
options.C: The electricity demand from local data centers has exceeded the grid's capacity.
options.D: The nuclear reactors are physically damaged by the high temperatures of the surrounding air.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，法國的核電廠依賴河水冷卻系統，但環保法規禁止將過熱的水排回河中，以免破壞生態。選項 A 文章未提及人力不足；選項 C 雖然提到 AI 資料中心耗電，但並非核電廠限產的直接原因；選項 D 錯誤，是因為河水過熱導致冷卻效率下降，而非反應爐本身被氣溫損壞。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what is the 'difficult cycle' mentioned in the third paragraph?

options.A: AI companies are building data centers faster than the energy grid can provide electricity.
options.B: Nuclear power plants are shutting down, forcing people to rely on more expensive energy sources.
options.C: Rising temperatures increase the demand for cooling while simultaneously reducing the capacity to generate electricity.
options.D: Extreme weather events like typhoons are causing more frequent power outages in Europe and Asia.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第三段指出，隨著氣溫升高，人們使用冷氣導致電力需求增加，但同時高溫又限制了核電廠的發電能力，這種「需求上升」與「供給受限」同時發生的矛盾即為該段提到的困難循環。選項 A 雖然是背景事實，但非該段落描述的循環；選項 B 和 D 均非文章中描述的特定循環機制。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by the phrase 'the challenge is two-fold'?

options.A: The challenge consists of two distinct parts that need to be addressed.
options.B: The challenge is twice as difficult as it was in previous years.
options.C: The challenge is being faced by two main groups: tech companies and governments.
options.D: The challenge involves two different types of energy: nuclear and renewable.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在文中，「two-fold」意指「雙重的」或「兩個面向的」。作者隨後列出了兩個具體任務：第一是提升 AI 的能源效率，第二是確保電網在氣候變遷下的穩定性。選項 B 誤解了該詞的量化含義；選項 C 雖然提到了相關群體，但並非該詞的定義；選項 D 則過度解讀了文章內容。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding AI and the environment?

options.A: The growth of AI is primarily responsible for the recent, record-breaking heatwaves in Europe.
options.B: Tech companies should stop building data centers until climate change is fully under control.
options.C: AI infrastructure faces significant sustainability challenges due to its high energy needs and a changing climate.
options.D: Nuclear power is the only reliable solution for the energy demands of modern technology.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章主旨探討了 AI 數位發展與實體環境限制（如能源需求、氣候變遷與極端天氣）之間的衝突。選項 A 因果倒置，AI 消耗能源但非氣候變遷的主因；選項 B 過於激進，文章並未建議停止建設；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未宣稱核能是唯一解，而是強調能源系統需更具韌性與永續性。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
